name: Fraction
javadoc:
/** * {@link Fraction} is a {@link Number} implementation that stores fractions accurately. *

* This class is immutable, and interoperable with most methods that accept a {@link Number}. *

*

* Note that this class is intended for common use cases, it is int based and thus suffers from various overflow issues. For a BigInteger based * equivalent, please see the Commons Math BigFraction class. *

* * @since 2.0 */

members.name: getFraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Creates a {@link Fraction} instance from a {@code double} value. *

* This method uses the continued fraction * algorithm, computing a maximum of 25 convergents and bounding the denominator by 10,000. *

* * @param value the double value to convert * @return a new fraction instance that is close to the value * @throws ArithmeticException if {@code |value| > Integer.MAX_VALUE} or {@code value = NaN} * @throws ArithmeticException if the calculated denominator is {@code zero} * @throws ArithmeticException if the algorithm does not converge */

tags.name: value
tags.content: the double value to convert
new fraction instance that is close to the value

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if {@code |value| > Integer.MAX_VALUE} or {@code value = NaN}

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if the calculated denominator is {@code zero}

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if the algorithm does not converge

members.name: getFraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Creates a {@link Fraction} instance with the 2 parts of a fraction Y/Z. *

* Any negative signs are resolved to be on the numerator. *

* * @param numerator the numerator, for example the three in 'three sevenths' * @param denominator the denominator, for example the seven in 'three sevenths' * @return a new fraction instance * @throws ArithmeticException if the denominator is {@code zero} or the denominator is {@code negative} and the numerator is {@code Integer#MIN_VALUE} */

tags.name: numerator
tags.content: the numerator, for example the three in 'three sevenths'

tags.name: denominator
tags.content: the denominator, for example the seven in 'three sevenths'
new fraction instance

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if the denominator is {@code zero} or the denominator is {@code negative} and the numerator is {@code Integer#MIN_VALUE}

members.name: getFraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Creates a {@link Fraction} instance with the 3 parts of a fraction X Y/Z. *

* The negative sign must be passed in on the whole number part. *

* * @param whole the whole number, for example the one in 'one and three sevenths' * @param numerator the numerator, for example the three in 'one and three sevenths' * @param denominator the denominator, for example the seven in 'one and three sevenths' * @return a new fraction instance * @throws ArithmeticException if the denominator is {@code zero} * @throws ArithmeticException if the denominator is negative * @throws ArithmeticException if the numerator is negative * @throws ArithmeticException if the resulting numerator exceeds {@code Integer.MAX_VALUE} */

tags.name: whole
tags.content: the whole number, for example the one in 'one and three sevenths'

tags.name: numerator
tags.content: the numerator, for example the three in 'one and three sevenths'

tags.name: denominator
tags.content: the denominator, for example the seven in 'one and three sevenths'
new fraction instance

tags.content: if the denominator is negative

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if the numerator is negative

members.name: getFraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Creates a Fraction from a {@link String}. *

* The formats accepted are: *

*
    *
  1. {@code double} String containing a dot
    2. *
  2. 'X Y/Z'
    3. *
  3. 'Y/Z'
    4. *
  4. 'X' (a simple whole number)
    5. *
*

* and a . *

* * @param str the string to parse, must not be {@code null} * @return the new {@link Fraction} instance * @throws NullPointerException if the string is {@code null} * @throws NumberFormatException if the number format is invalid */

tags.name: str
tags.content: the string to parse, must not be {@code null}
new {@link Fraction} instance

tags.name: NullPointerException
tags.content: if the string is {@code null}

tags.name: NumberFormatException
tags.content: if the number format is invalid

members.name: getReducedFraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Creates a reduced {@link Fraction} instance with the 2 parts of a fraction Y/Z. *

* For example, if the input parameters represent 2/4, then the created fraction will be 1/2. *

* *

* Any negative signs are resolved to be on the numerator. *

* * @param numerator the numerator, for example the three in 'three sevenths' * @param denominator the denominator, for example the seven in 'three sevenths' * @return a new fraction instance, with the numerator and denominator reduced * @throws ArithmeticException if the denominator is {@code zero} */

tags.name: numerator
tags.content: the numerator, for example the three in 'three sevenths'

tags.name: denominator
tags.content: the denominator, for example the seven in 'three sevenths'
new fraction instance, with the numerator and denominator reduced

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if the denominator is {@code zero}

members.name: abs
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets a fraction that is the positive equivalent of this one. *

* More precisely: {@code (fraction >= 0 ? this : -fraction)} *

*

* The returned fraction is not reduced. *

* * @return {@code this} if it is positive, or a new positive fraction instance with the opposite signed numerator */

tags.content: this} if it is positive, or a new positive fraction instance with the opposite signed numerator

members.name: compareTo
members.javadoc:
/** * Compares this object to another based on size. *

* Note: this class has a natural ordering that is inconsistent with equals, because, for example, equals treats 1/2 and 2/4 as different, whereas compareTo * treats them as equal. *

* * @param other the object to compare to * @return -1 if this is less, 0 if equal, +1 if greater * @throws ClassCastException if the object is not a {@link Fraction} * @throws NullPointerException if the object is {@code null} */

tags.name: other
tags.content: the object to compare to
if this is less, 0 if equal, +1 if greater

tags.name: NullPointerException
tags.content: if the object is {@code null}

members.name: equals
members.javadoc:
/** * Compares this fraction to another object to test if they are equal. *

* To be equal, both values must be equal. Thus 2/4 is not equal to 1/2. *

* * @param obj the reference object with which to compare * @return {@code true} if this object is equal */

tags.name: obj
tags.content: the reference object with which to compare
true} if this object is equal

members.name: getDenominator
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the denominator part of the fraction.
 *
 * @return the denominator fraction part
 */

tags.content: denominator fraction part

members.name: getNumerator
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the numerator part of the fraction. *

* This method may return a value greater than the denominator, an improper fraction, such as the seven in 7/4. *

* * @return the numerator fraction part */

tags.content: numerator fraction part

members.name: getProperNumerator
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the proper numerator, always positive. *

* An improper fraction 7/4 can be resolved into a proper one, 1 3/4. This method returns the 3 from the proper fraction. *

* *

* If the fraction is negative such as -7/4, it can be resolved into -1 3/4, so this method returns the positive proper numerator, 3. *

* * @return the numerator fraction part of a proper fraction, always positive */

tags.content: numerator fraction part of a proper fraction, always positive

members.code:
/** * Gets the proper numerator, always positive. *

* An improper fraction 7/4 can be resolved into a proper one, 1 3/4. This method returns the 3 from the proper fraction. *

* *

* If the fraction is negative such as -7/4, it can be resolved into -1 3/4, so this method returns the positive proper numerator, 3. *

* * @return the numerator fraction part of a proper fraction, always positive */ public int getProperNumerator() { return Math.abs(numerator % denominator); }

members.name: getProperWhole
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the proper whole part of the fraction. *

* An improper fraction 7/4 can be resolved into a proper one, 1 3/4. This method returns the 1 from the proper fraction. *

* *

* If the fraction is negative such as -7/4, it can be resolved into -1 3/4, so this method returns the positive whole part -1. *

* * @return the whole fraction part of a proper fraction, that includes the sign */

tags.content: whole fraction part of a proper fraction, that includes the sign

members.code:
/** * Gets the proper whole part of the fraction. *

* An improper fraction 7/4 can be resolved into a proper one, 1 3/4. This method returns the 1 from the proper fraction. *

* *

* If the fraction is negative such as -7/4, it can be resolved into -1 3/4, so this method returns the positive whole part -1. *

* * @return the whole fraction part of a proper fraction, that includes the sign */ public int getProperWhole() { return numerator / denominator; }

/** * Gets a fraction that is the inverse (1/fraction) of this one. *

* The returned fraction is not reduced. *

* * @return a new fraction instance with the numerator and denominator inverted. * @throws ArithmeticException if the fraction represents zero. */

tags.content: new fraction instance with the numerator and denominator inverted.

tags.name: ArithmeticException
tags.content: if the fraction represents zero.

members.name: pow
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets a fraction that is raised to the passed in power. *

* The returned fraction is in reduced form. *

* * @param power the power to raise the fraction to * @return {@code this} if the power is one, {@link #ONE} if the power is zero (even if the fraction equals ZERO) or a new fraction instance raised to the * appropriate power * @throws ArithmeticException if the resulting numerator or denominator exceeds {@code Integer.MAX_VALUE} */

tags.name: power
tags.content: the power to raise the fraction to
this} if the power is one, {@link #ONE} if the power is zero (even if the fraction equals ZERO) or a new fraction instance raised to the appropriate power

members.name: reduce
members.javadoc:
/** * Reduce the fraction to the smallest values for the numerator and denominator, returning the result. *

* For example, if this fraction represents 2/4, then the result will be 1/2. *

* * @return a new reduced fraction instance, or this if no simplification possible */

tags.content: new reduced fraction instance, or this if no simplification possible

members.name: toProperString
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the fraction as a proper {@link String} in the format X Y/Z. *

* The format used in 'wholeNumber numerator/denominator'. If the whole number is zero it will be omitted. If the numerator is * zero, only the whole number is returned. *

* * @return a {@link String} form of the fraction */

tags.content: {@link String} form of the fraction

members.name: toString
members.javadoc:
/** * Gets the fraction as a {@link String}. *

* The format used is 'numerator/denominator' always. *

* * @return a {@link String} form of the fraction */

tags.content: {@link String} form of the fraction
